commands.review_summary:
The branch restores native Codex memory recall guidance by activating the configured memory capability for tool-enabled Codex turns and adds a private bundled-Codex plugin-SDK helper, docs, package inventory updates, and focused tests.

PR surface: Source +246, Tests +328, Docs +3, Config +2, Other +3. Total +582 across 19 files.

Reproducibility: yes. by source inspection: current main routes MEMORY.md through a pointer when tools are available but does not activate or render the configured memory capability's recall guidance for Codex turns.
commands.review_followup: - The PR is still draft and touches native Codex prompt behavior plus private SDK packaging, so it needs maintainer review and CI rather than an automated repair.

target.title: fix(codex): restore memory recall guidance
target.body:
## Summary

- Ensure native Codex app-server turns activate the configured memory capability before rendering memory recall guidance.
- Keep #87383's intended root `MEMORY.md` guard: raw workspace memory is still excluded when it can be routed through tools, and retained when the effective workspace differs.
- Do not add a Codex-local fallback memory policy. Codex uses the active memory engine/tool contract instead of inventing recall instructions.
- Add regression coverage for the two observed failure modes: memory tools available while root memory was not routeable, and a compatible plugin registry present while the memory capability/prompt hooks were absent.
- AI-assisted: yes. I understand what the code changes and validated it below.

## Linked context

Related: #87383

This fixes a regression introduced by #87383. That PR correctly stopped pasting raw native Codex workspace `MEMORY.md` into ordinary tool-enabled turns, but Codex tied memory recall guidance to the separate question of whether root workspace `MEMORY.md` could be routed through tools. In a real Telegram/Codex run, `memory_search` and `memory_get` were visible as deferred tools, but the first recommendation skipped saved credit-card constraints.

The ownership boundary in this PR is narrow: the memory engine/tool contract owns recall behavior; Codex ensures that capability is active and then forwards/uses the registered memory behavior when memory tools are available. The activation helper is exposed through the private local-only `plugin-sdk/codex-memory-runtime` helper for bundled Codex, not through the public `plugin-sdk/memory-host-search` facade.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior addressed: Native Codex turns could receive searchable memory tools without using the active memory engine's recall behavior, so personalized recommendations could skip saved user constraints until corrected later.
- Before evidence: Devbox gateway from an earlier PR branch head before this fix, Telegram direct route, `memory-core` + `codex` + `telegram` loaded from that checkout's `dist`.
- Exact steps or command run before this patch: Built the checkout with `pnpm build`, restarted the gateway from `/home/rubencu/workplace/openclaw-codex-memory-recall`, sent the Telegram prompt `I am about to meet the SUB of the Amex Platinum. What card should I get next?`, and inspected the resulting runtime trajectory.
- Evidence before fix (redacted runtime trace):

```text
2026-06-09T06:54:25Z telegram inbound: [redacted user/chat] "I am about to meet the SUB of the Amex Platinum. What card should I get next?"
rollout=/home/rubencu/.openclaw/agents/main/agent/codex-home/sessions/2026/06/09/rollout-2026-06-09T06-54-28-019eab29-409b-76d2-967e-8e683dcbcfbf.jsonl
trajectory=/home/rubencu/.openclaw/agents/main/sessions/b28b87c0-2982-4a0c-b04d-b4a9c7100343.trajectory.jsonl

prompt/tool summary:
  memory_search available=true
  memory_get available=true
  tool_search available=true
  Memory Recall section=false
  Codex Memory Tool Loading section=false

tool calls observed:
  web_search=10
  tool_search=0
  memory_search=0
  memory_get=0

visible answer still hedged on 5/24 instead of applying the saved card-opening history.
```

- Real environment tested: Devbox OpenClaw gateway from the PR source tree at `3d9b4ffc1ba4ae740f14e2d48489e2f8fbf9db3b`, launched from `/home/rubencu/workplace/openclaw-codex-memory-recall/dist/index.js` with `brave`, `codex`, `memory-core`, and `telegram` loaded for the same Telegram direct route. Telegram bot name, user/chat ids, and auth/profile ids are redacted. Current head `fa00e77a95c18691a3ebe9726f46837a4d7e3d78` contains the SDK-boundary cleanup for the same runtime behavior and was validated with the tests/build checks below; live Telegram was not rerun after that boundary-only cleanup.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Built runtime artifacts with `node scripts/build-all.mjs qaRuntime`, built/checked plugin SDK declarations with `pnpm build:plugin-sdk:dts && node --experimental-strip-types scripts/write-plugin-sdk-entry-dts.ts && node scripts/check-plugin-sdk-exports.mjs`, restarted `openclaw-gateway-pr-memory-recall.service`, sent the same Telegram prompt, and inspected the Codex rollout/trajectory.
- Evidence after fix:

```text
Live proof head: 3d9b4ffc1ba4ae740f14e2d48489e2f8fbf9db3b
service: openclaw-gateway-pr-memory-recall.service
command: /usr/local/bin/node /home/rubencu/workplace/openclaw-codex-memory-recall/dist/index.js gateway --port 18789

2026-06-09T07:44:46Z [gateway] http server listening (4 plugins: brave, codex, memory-core, telegram; 7.4s)
2026-06-09T07:44:46Z [gateway] ready
2026-06-09T07:44:47Z [telegram] [default] starting provider ([redacted Telegram bot])
2026-06-09T07:44:56Z [gateway] agent runtime plugins pre-warmed in 16ms

2026-06-09T11:17:16Z telegram inbound: [redacted user/chat] "I am about to meet the SUB of the Amex Platinum. What card should I get next?"
rollout=/home/rubencu/.openclaw/agents/main/agent/codex-home/sessions/2026/06/09/rollout-2026-06-09T11-17-19-019eac19-e775-7fd0-a701-06e81464c203.jsonl
trajectory=/home/rubencu/.openclaw/agents/main/sessions/2c02a9de-9c43-4368-957a-1cefb4a61971.trajectory.jsonl

first relevant rollout calls:
  line 9:  tool_search query="memory_search memory_get OpenClaw memory tools"
  line 12: memory_search query="credit card preferences Amex Platinum Chase 5/24 points travel airline hotel Ruben" corpus=all maxResults=6
  line 16: memory_get path="memory/2026-05-20.md" from=1 lines=22 corpus=memory
  line 23: message says prior note was found: already has CSP and is probably well over Chase 5/24
  line 72: final recommendation: Capital One Venture Rewards; Chase treated as off the table due to 5/24

memory_get returned the saved note:
  "Current cards he listed: Bilt Blue, Amex Gold, Bank of America Unlimited Cash Rewards, Iberia Visa Signature, British Airways Visa Signature, Chase Sapphire Preferred, Wells Fargo Autograph."
  "After Amex Platinum acceptance on May 20, 2026, count is 8/24 as of June 6, 2026; Chase apps should be treated as off the board until enough accounts age out."
```

- Observed result after fix: In the same Telegram user path, Codex discovered and used `memory_search`/`memory_get` before making the card recommendation, applied the saved Chase 5/24/CSP constraints from memory on the first recommendation, and avoided the previous initial Sapphire/Chase recommendation path.
- What was not tested: Non-Codex harnesses were not live-tested. Full `pnpm build` was attempted twice on the devbox, but `tsdown` was SIGKILLed during the DTS-heavy full build with no TypeScript or bundler diagnostic; runtime build plus plugin SDK declaration/export checks passed separately.

## Tests and validation

Current PR head validated: `fa00e77a95c18691a3ebe9726f46837a4d7e3d78`.

- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/plugin-sdk/codex-memory-runtime.ts src/plugin-sdk/memory-host-search.ts src/plugin-sdk/memory-host-search.runtime.ts src/plugin-sdk/memory-host-search.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/attempt-context.ts src/plugins/sdk-alias.ts src/plugins/sdk-alias.test.ts src/plugins/plugin-sdk-dist-alias.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `node scripts/check-plugin-sdk-subpath-exports.mjs`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugin-sdk/memory-host-search.test.ts src/plugins/memory-runtime.test.ts src/plugins/sdk-alias.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/attempt-context.test.ts` - 188 tests passed
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-subpaths.test.ts src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-index.test.ts src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-root-alias.test.ts src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-package-contract-guardrails.test.ts` - 75 tests passed
- `pnpm build:plugin-sdk:dts && node --experimental-strip-types scripts/write-plugin-sdk-entry-dts.ts && node scripts/check-plugin-sdk-exports.mjs`
- `pnpm tsgo:prod`
- `pnpm tsgo:test`
- `node scripts/build-all.mjs qaRuntime`

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? `Yes`

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? `No`

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? `No`

What is the highest-risk area? Prompt/tool discovery behavior for native Codex memory routing.

How is that risk mitigated? The change activates the configured memory capability through a private bundled-Codex helper, preserves #87383's root `MEMORY.md` routing guard, keeps the no-memory-slot case empty, and adds focused coverage for the routeability-false case and compatible-registry-with-missing-capability case that reproduced on the devbox.

## Current review state

Next action: maintainer review and PR CI.

Waiting on: PR CI.

Bot/reviewer comments addressed: The previous concern about a Codex-local fallback is addressed by this head; there is no hardcoded fallback recall policy in Codex. The ClawSweeper SDK-boundary concern is addressed by moving activation off the public `memory-host-search` facade into a private local-only bundled Codex helper.
target.author: rubencu
